summary: The test suite covers all seven spec-required security paths (replay rejection, sig recovery tamper matrix, malformed payload, chain mismatch, vault_not_deployed guard, relayer key opacity, settlement taxonomy). Three security gaps require fixes before this round closes: (1) the ChallengeBody schema uses `additionalProperties: true`, undermining the strict-shape drift detection it is supposed to provide; (2) the D18 `nonce_replay_attempt` event name wired in `core.ts` is `nonce_replay` but the task spec mandates `nonce_replay_attempt`, and no test pins the canonical event name against the tech-spec table; (3) there is no test asserting that `payTo === ZERO_ADDRESS` short-circuits BEFORE `verify` is invoked when factory state is served stale with a zero vault — the `vault_not_deployed_short_circuits_BEFORE_verify` test only covers the non-stale path. No Critical or High-severity bugs found in the production source modules reviewed.

findings.title: ChallengeBody schema uses `additionalProperties: true` — drift in 402 response fields is not caught
findings.description: The `ChallengeBody` definition sets `additionalProperties: true`. This means that the ajv validation in `x402.test.ts` and `errors.test.ts` will pass for any object that contains `x402Version:1` and `accepts:[]`, regardless of what other fields are present. The stated purpose of the schema fixture (from task spec §1) is to catch future *drift from the wire format* at unit-test time. That guarantee is not provided for the 402 body: a regression that adds an undocumented top-level field (e.g., typo `eroor` alongside `error`) would silently pass validation. By contrast, `PaymentRequirements`, `ExactEvmPayload`, `PaymentPayload`, and `XPaymentResponse` all correctly use `additionalProperties: false`.
findings.impact: If a future change adds an undocumented field to the 402 challenge body (e.g., renaming `reason` to `settlementReason` in some error paths), the schema test will not catch the divergence from the x402 v1 spec. The test provides false confidence about wire-format compliance for the 402 body shape.
findings.recommendation: Change `additionalProperties: true` to `additionalProperties: false` in the `ChallengeBody` definition. The current allowed properties (`x402Version`, `accepts`, `error`, `reason`, `settlementReason`, `required`, `received`) are already enumerated in the schema's `properties` block — locking `additionalProperties` to false simply enforces that no unknown fields sneak in. If there are legitimate extension fields (e.g., the `reason` sub-field used for settlement failure), make them part of `properties` explicitly (they already are).

findings.title: D18 `nonce_replay_attempt` event name mismatch: `core.ts` emits `nonce_replay` but no test pins canonical name against spec
findings.description: The tech-spec D18 event catalog (tech-spec.md) defines the event name as `nonce_replay_attempt`. The `core.ts` implementation emits `nonce_replay` (line 539: `emit(opts, 'nonce_replay', {...})`). The `core.test.ts` parameterized test at line 579-600 maps `'nonce_already_used'` → `'nonce_replay'` — that is, the test is written to match the *current implementation* rather than the *spec*. Neither the test nor the source anchors to the canonical name from the D18 table. When a SIEM or security monitoring system is configured against the canonical event catalog (`nonce_replay_attempt`), it will not receive any events because the wire name is `nonce_replay`. This is a naming-contract violation between the implementation and the tech-spec.
findings.impact: Security monitoring that subscribes to `nonce_replay_attempt` (per tech-spec D18) receives no events. Replay attacks that are caught by the middleware go undetected in alerting systems. The test suite masks the bug by asserting the wrong string.
findings.recommendation: 1. In `types.ts` (or wherever `SecurityEventName` is defined), rename `'nonce_replay'` to `'nonce_replay_attempt'` to match the tech-spec D18 catalog. 2. Update `core.ts` line 539 and any related switch/case. 3. In `core.test.ts`, update the parameterized array to map `'nonce_already_used'` → `'nonce_replay_attempt'`. 4. Add a source-level test (similar to the `source-level emission ownership` suite at the bottom of `core.test.ts`) that reads `types.ts` or the event catalog and asserts that every name in the D18 table is present verbatim — so future renames are caught at test-time against the spec document.

findings.title: No test for the stale-cache + zero-vault path that must short-circuit BEFORE verify
findings.description: The task spec (task 9, TDD anchor `core.test.ts::vault_not_deployed_returns_402`) mandates that when `factory.vaults(eoa) === 0x0`, the 402 `vault_not_deployed` response fires BEFORE `verifyEip3009Authorization` is called. The commit correctly includes a test for this at line 272 (`vault_not_deployed short-circuits BEFORE verify is called`). However, the stale-cache code path in `core.ts` (lines 492–511) has a distinct sub-path: when the pre-request factory-state read returns `servedStale: true` and the second factory-state re-read ALSO returns `servedStale: true`, the code surfaces `rpc_5xx` and returns early — that path never reaches `verify`. This is correct behavior. But the stale-cache scenario where the second read SUCCEEDS and returns `vaults = ZERO_ADDRESS` (vault not yet deployed, cache was stale) is not covered by any test. In that sub-case `verify` must still not be called. Without a test, a future refactor could accidentally call `verify` with `expectedVaultAddress = ZERO_ADDRESS` — the ZERO_ADDRESS short-circuit guard would be bypassed, and an attacker crafting `authorization.to = 0x0` would pass the `to_mismatch` check.
findings.impact: Low as-written (the production code handles it correctly), but the absence of this test leaves a regression trap. The SEC-T8-05 finding from the prior review wave was exactly this attack vector — the guard was added in `core.ts` lines 492-519 but the test only covers one of two paths into that guard.
findings.recommendation: Add a test: `stale_cache_then_vault_not_deployed_still_short_circuits_before_verify`. Warm the cache, advance timers past TTL, configure `readContract` to return `{paused: false, vaults: ZERO_ADDRESS}` on the second call (so `servedStale: false`), send a request with a crafted payload where `authorization.to = ZERO_ADDRESS`, and assert that `verifySpy` was NOT called.

tests: core.test.ts::replay-store entry is retained after settlement failure
replay_rejection.notes: TOCTOU-safe `checkAndInsert` primitive is correctly pinned. Replay-store retention after settlement failure is explicitly asserted through the core orchestrator with the module-scope NonceStore singleton. Strong coverage.

tests: verify.test.ts::malformed signature surfaces as invalid_signature (new in this commit)
signature_recovery.notes: Full EIP-712 domain tamper matrix covered. The new `malformed signature` test (0xff*65 blob) exercises the `recoverTypedDataAddress throws → catch → invalid_signature` branch which was previously uncovered. The test for `default nowMs` correctly covers the fallback to `Date.now()`. Strong coverage.

tests: core.test.ts::malformed base64 (pre-existing), malformed JSON, wrong shape
malformed_payload_rejection.notes: Good expansion. Decimal-integer-string guard for `validAfter`/`validBefore` and non-string network are now explicitly tested. The `malformedShapeHeader` helper in `encode-header.ts` is available but not directly used in `core.test.ts` (it constructs them inline) — minor inconsistency, not a security gap.

tests: settle.test.ts::unknown network throws plain Error referencing the network key (new)
chain_mismatch.notes: The new `unknown network` test in `settle.test.ts` correctly pins the `throw new Error(...)` path (not `NetworkMismatchError`) for an unconfigured network key. The `unknown expected network` in `verify.test.ts` covers the defensive `network_mismatch` return for a misconfigured `expectedNetwork`. Chain-replay attack vectors (domain.chainId tamper) covered in verify tamper matrix. Coverage is thorough.

tests: core.test.ts::vault_not_deployed short-circuits BEFORE verify is called
vault_not_deployed_before_verify.notes: Covers the non-stale path. Missing: stale-cache + successful refresh + zero-vault path (SEC-T9-03).

tests: relayer-key.test.ts::throws when called on a brand-forged object with no associated secret (new)
core.test.ts::core passes OpaqueRelayerKey opaque to settle — never extracts
relayer_key_opacity.notes: New brand-forging test exercises the `SECRET_TABLE.get(key) === undefined` defensive branch. Core-level test validates the OpaqueRelayerKey is passed by reference without extraction. Strong defense-in-depth coverage.

tests: settle.test.ts::balanceOf RPC throws viem TimeoutError → rpc_timeout (new)
core.test.ts::settle failure with reason %s (parameterized over all 7)
settlement_taxonomy.notes: New test for balance-read timeout → `rpc_timeout` covers the path where `readContract` throws `TimeoutError` before `writeContract` is ever called (distinct code path from the `writeContract` timeout). Mine_timeout vs rpc_timeout distinction is maintained.

positive_observations: SEC-T8-05 guard (vault_not_deployed before verify) is tested and the implementation correctly places the factory-state check before the EIP-712 recovery call.
The `sign.ts` helper uses a well-identified fake private key (0x11...12) that is obviously synthetic and scoped to vitest — no risk of accidental production use.
The `RecordingLogger` captures typed event payloads without crossing secret material boundaries — `securityEvent` receives only the scrubbed event payload.
Source-level ownership test (`verify.ts` and `settle.ts` do not import SecurityLogger) structurally prevents a whole class of double-emission bugs regardless of future refactors.
The `emit` helper carve-out for `txHash` (SAFE_HEX_FIELDS) is tested at the structural level and the baseline is pinned (scrubSecrets WOULD redact it without the carve-out).

The `forged-brand` test for `getRelayerKeySecret` is a high-value defensive test — it covers the WeakMap-miss branch that cannot be reached through normal usage but could be triggered by cross-realm or prototype-pollution attacks.
Coverage thresholds (85% lines, 85% functions, 75% branches) enforced at CI level — `vitest.config.ts` threshold block will cause CI failure on regression.
The ajv schema is loaded once at module scope (not re-parsed per test) — no performance issue from schema validation tests.
required_fixes: SEC-T9-02 (Medium): Rename `nonce_replay` to `nonce_replay_attempt` in `types.ts`, `core.ts`, and `core.test.ts` to align with D18 canonical event catalog.
SEC-T9-03 (Low): Add missing test for stale-cache + successful-refresh + zero-vault path to confirm `verify` is not called with `expectedVaultAddress=ZERO_ADDRESS`.
conditional_pass_rationale: No Critical or High findings. The three findings are: one schema configuration bug (SEC-T9-01) that weakens drift detection but does not cause incorrect production behavior; one event naming mismatch (SEC-T9-02) that breaks security monitoring integration; one test coverage gap (SEC-T9-03) for a known-guarded code path. The test suite can proceed to round 2 once these three items are addressed. The core security invariants (replay rejection, EIP-712 tamper matrix, relayer key opacity, settlement taxonomy, vault guard before verify) are all well-covered.
